description: Fix blob URL and DOM element leak in download-attachment.ts where a 1-second setTimeout cleanup is skipped if the page navigates before the timer fires.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/outlook && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Revoke blob URL synchronously after click in triggerBrowserDownload
userStories.description: In plugins/outlook/src/tools/download-attachment.ts, triggerBrowserDownload() appends an <a> element and calls URL.revokeObjectURL() inside a setTimeout(..., 1000) callback. If the page navigates within that 1-second window, the setTimeout is cancelled and the blob URL is never revoked, leaking memory proportional to attachment size. Modern browsers support calling URL.revokeObjectURL() immediately after a.click() since the download is already initiated. Revoke synchronously after the click and remove the DOM element immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: URL.revokeObjectURL(url) is called synchronously after a.click(), not inside setTimeout
document.body.removeChild(a) is called synchronously after a.click(), not inside setTimeout
The download still triggers correctly (a.click() still initiates the download before cleanup)
